title: BlackBerry Q4 & FY2026 Earnings Summary
text: BlackBerry achieved significant milestones in fiscal 2026, returning to growth after previous declines. Q4 Revenue: $156.0 million, up 10% year-over-year demonstrates renewed momentum. Full-year revenue reached $549.1 million, representing 3% year-over-year growth. This turnaround reflects improved execution following leadership changes in 2023. The company delivered impressive profit improvements with Q4 Net Income: GAAP $24.3 million (eighth consecutive quarter of improvement). For the full year, BlackBerry swung from a prior loss of $79.0 million to GAAP net income of $53.2 million. Q4 Adjusted EBITDA: $36.1 million, up 71% YoY, while full-year adjusted EBITDA reached $107.1 million, up 27% year-over-year. Operating cash flow strengthened considerably with the company generating $50.3 million in operating cash flow for FY2026, compared to $16.5 million the prior year. Total cash and investments reached $432.4 million as of February 28, 2026. BlackBerry returned capital to shareholders through $60 million share buybacks while maintaining financial flexibility. The QNX flagship segment achieved record quarterly revenue of $78.7 million (up 20% YoY) with an impressive 84% adjusted gross margin. The royalty backlog grew to $950 million, providing future revenue visibility. The Secure Communications segment returned to revenue growth with $72.5 million in Q4 revenue, up 8% year-over-year, supported by government and defense demand.
